description_long: This object is a vintage computer terminal with an integrated monitor and keyboard, reminiscent of the Commodore PET series. The monitor is encased in a boxy, angular housing with a matte, metallic finish, and features a large, slightly recessed display area. The front panel below the screen displays branding and model information, with the keyboard embedded into the sloped lower section. The keyboard consists of numerous small, dark keys arranged in a grid, and the overall design is utilitarian with minimal decorative elements. The rear and sides of the housing are plain, with sharp edges and a uniform color. The object appears robust and heavy, typical of early personal computers.
description: A vintage, boxy computer terminal with an integrated monitor and keyboard, featuring a matte metallic finish and prominent branding on the front panel.
description_view_0: Side view showing the angular, boxy housing and the sloped keyboard section extending from the base.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the large display, branding, and the full keyboard layout beneath the screen.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the angular housing and sloped keyboard, with minimal surface details.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the plain, flat back of the monitor housing with no visible ports or features.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, flat, and angular, made of matte plastic with a uniform gray color and no decorative elements or rounded corners.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by an integrated, sloped base that merges seamlessly with the keyboard section, forming a single, solid unit with no visible adjustability.
attribute_button_placement: Physical buttons are not distinctly visible; all interactive elements appear to be on the keyboard, which is positioned directly below the screen.
attribute_brand_logo: A brand logo and model information are present on the front panel below the screen, centered and printed in white text.
